steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Two independent clauses must be joined correctly: one about the Longyearbyen observatory in Norway and one about Buckland Park in Australia. The blank determines the punctuation between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — no punctuation or wrong connector: creates a run-on sentence by failing to separate the two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice B — comma alone: a comma splice — a comma cannot join two independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction. ✗
  • Choice C — misplaced conjunction: uses a connector that distorts the logical relationship between the two clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — semicolon: a semicolon correctly separates two independent clauses. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
